message:
fix(freeze): convert bytecode atoms to runtime atoms for getters/setters

Fixes Zod validation library support - all 10 tests now pass.

The issue: define_method opcode was using bytecode atom indices directly
(e.g., 330 for "x") instead of runtime atoms. Bytecode atoms are
module-local indices, while QuickJS runtime uses different global atom
numbering. When property was accessed with getPropertyStr("x"), QuickJS
looked up the runtime atom which didn't match the bytecode atom.

Changes:
- zig_codegen_full.zig: define_method now uses JS_NewAtom(ctx, prop_name)
  to create runtime atoms for getter/setter property definitions
- native_dispatch.zig: Use "anonymous" fallback for empty function names
  (getters/setters in object literals have no name)
- Also fixes vstack handling for dup opcode and stack reference updates
